2605.21086.title: LoCar: Localization-Aware Evaluation of In-Vehicle Assistants through Fine-Grained Sociolinguistic Control
2605.21086.paper_summary: While Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into in-vehicle conversational systems, identifying the optimal model remains challenging due to the lack of domain-specific evaluation standards tailored to real-world deployment requirements. In this paper, we propose a novel evaluation framework for in-vehicle assistants, with a particular focus on Korean-language localization. Our empirical analysis reveals notable patterns in model behavior. First, fine-grained Korean honorific control remains unstable in current LLMs, indicating that precise speech-level realization must be explicitly evaluated in localization settings. Second, models exhibit weaker performance in strategic conversational metrics like clarification and proactivity. Our analysis suggests this stems from the inherent subjective complexity of these tasks, where our framework adopts a conservative evaluation stance to prioritize reliability. Together, our findings underscore that automotive AI must move beyond general competence toward precise linguistic tailoring and reliable, safety-oriented interaction management.
2605.21086.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被整合進車載對話系統，但由於缺乏針對現實部署需求量身定制的領域特定評估標準，識別最佳模型仍然具有挑戰性。在本文中，我們提出了一個針對車載助手的新評估框架，特別關注韓語本地化。我們的實證分析揭示了模型行為中的顯著模式。首先，目前的LLMs在細緻的韓語敬語控制方面仍然不穩定，這表明精確的語音層次實現必須在本地化環境中明確評估。其次，模型在澄清和主動性等戰略對話指標上的表現較弱。我們的分析表明，這源於這些任務固有的主觀複雜性，我們的框架採取保守的評估立場，以優先考慮可靠性。綜合來看，我們的發現強調汽車人工智慧必須超越一般能力，朝向精確的語言調整和可靠的安全導向互動管理邁進。
2605.21086.author: Seogyeong Jeong et.al.
2605.21086.authors: Seogyeong Jeong, Kiwoong Park, Seyoung Song, Eunsu Kim, Ken E. Friedl, Jaeho Kim, Alice Oh
